summary: Split Bowser/Koopa decomp code out of the previous ftKp_Init/ftKp_SpecialS/ftKp_Unk1 layout into linked matching objects for SpecialS, SpecialLw, SpecialN, and SpecialHi. The PR updated GALE01 split ranges, changed configure.py from one nonmatching Koopa special object to four Matching objects, moved prototypes into per-special headers, deleted the tiny ftKp_Unk1 file, and added dependent includes/prototypes needed by ftdata, Giga Bowser, and Koopa flame item code. The decomp.dev bot reported +10780 linked bytes and +0.28% linked code, with 72 new matches mirrored by 72 broken matches because functions moved units rather than newly becoming matched.
what_changed: GALE01 splits were refined: ftKp_Init text now ends at 0x80132EFC; ftKp_SpecialS covers 0x80132EFC-0x80134518; ftKp_SpecialLw covers 0x80134518-0x80134ACC; ftKp_SpecialN covers 0x80134ACC-0x80135A2C with data 0x803CF2A0-0x803CF420; ftKp_SpecialHi covers 0x80135A2C-0x80136258.
configure.py removed ftKp_Unk1.c and replaced the old NonMatching ftKp_SpecialS.c entry with Matching entries for ftKp_SpecialS.c, ftKp_SpecialLw.c, ftKp_SpecialN.c, and ftKp_SpecialHi.c.
Large blocks of special-move code were moved out of ftKp_Init.c and the monolithic/overloaded ftKp_SpecialS.c into new ftKp_SpecialN.c, ftKp_SpecialLw.c, and ftKp_SpecialHi.c translation units.
ftKp_Init.h and ftKp_SpecialS.h were trimmed so they no longer declare unrelated SpecialN/SpecialLw/SpecialHi functions; new per-special headers carry those declarations.
ftKp_Unk1.c and ftKp_Unk1.h were deleted; their enum table ftKp_Init_803CF2A0 appears in the new ftKp_SpecialN.c unit, matching the new split's data placement.
ftGigaKoopa/ftGk_Init.c and ftdata.c now include the new Koopa special headers so moved symbols remain visible to cross-fighter/data-table users.
itkoopaflame.h gained a prototype for it_802ACBA0, which is called by the moved Koopa special code.

The decomp.dev report showed 72 new matches and 72 broken matches, indicating a unit relocation/split: examples moved from ftKp_Init or ftKp_SpecialS into ftKp_SpecialS, ftKp_SpecialLw, ftKp_SpecialN, and ftKp_SpecialHi units.
smart_moves: The split ranges were made address-accurate in splits.txt before marking the new objects Matching in configure.py, keeping linker configuration synchronized with source reorganization.

The PR did not force every function into a semantic file purely by function prefix: the data/text boundary at 0x80134ACC places ftKp_SpecialLw_80134ACC and related functions in the ftKp_SpecialN object, consistent with the original object layout reported by decomp.dev.
Header cleanup reduced accidental coupling: ftKp_Init.h stopped exporting numerous SpecialLw/SpecialN/SpecialHi declarations, and ftKp_SpecialS.h stopped carrying unrelated Lw/N declarations.
Cross-object dependencies were handled explicitly by adding includes in ftdata.c and ftGk_Init.c rather than relying on the old aggregated Init/SpecialS headers.
The deleted ftKp_Unk1 object was folded into the object that owns its data under the new split, avoiding an artificial standalone data file in configure.py.
The item-spawn dependency was made visible by declaring it_802ACBA0 in itkoopaflame.h, supporting the moved Koopa flame call without local extern hacks.
decomp_lessons: When splitting fighter files, update config/GALE01/splits.txt and configure.py together; a source move alone is not enough to produce linked matching objects.

Use original object boundaries, not just move semantics or function prefixes, to decide file ownership. This PR has SpecialLw-named functions/data at the beginning of the SpecialN object because the split boundary starts there.
After splitting a monolithic fighter source, prune headers aggressively so each special's header exports only the declarations consumers actually need.
If Giga variants or ftdata tables reference base-fighter special functions, add the new per-special headers to those users during the split.
Small unknown/data-only files can disappear once the correct owning object is identified; check data ranges in splits.txt before keeping placeholder Unk files.
A decomp.dev report with equal new and broken matches is expected for pure object/file relocation; judge whether linked bytes increased and whether all moved functions are matched in their new units.
naming_conventions: Koopa/Bowser files use the ftKp_ prefix and per-special file names: ftKp_SpecialS, ftKp_SpecialLw, ftKp_SpecialN, ftKp_SpecialHi.
Ground/air entrypoint pairs follow the established pattern ftKp_SpecialX_Enter and ftKp_SpecialAirX_Enter.
Callback suffixes remain standard fighter-action names such as _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Address-suffixed unknown helpers are retained when not yet named semantically, for example ftKp_SpecialS_8013302C, ftKp_SpecialN_80135780, and ftKp_SpecialLw_80134ACC.
Internal matching helpers use local static/static inline names such as ftKp_SpecialHi_Enter_inline, ftKp_SpecialN_Anim_inline_1, and fn_80134518 when evidence for better names is not present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used precise split start/end addresses to isolate original objects and allow each Koopa special file to be marked Matching.
Preserved matching-sensitive local structure with static inline helpers and PAD_STACK annotations in new files, especially SpecialHi, SpecialLw, and SpecialN code.
Kept transition flag constants as static u32 const values in ftKp_SpecialS.c, including notes to combine common flags later, rather than refactoring in ways that could perturb matching.
Moved prototypes/includes instead of changing call sites wholesale, limiting semantic edits while reorganizing translation units.
Accepted stub empty IASA functions where present in the matched code, e.g. ftKp_SpecialHi_IASA and several SpecialN IASA variants.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only recorded feedback was the decomp.dev bot report for GALE01: linked code increased to 28.33% (+0.28%, +10780 bytes), with 72 new matches and 72 broken matches caused by unit relocation.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftKp_SpecialS_8013302C, ftKp_SpecialN_80135780, ftKp_SpecialLw_80134ACC, fn_80134518, or fn_80134590.
Check future Koopa PRs for cleanup of TODOs around transition_flags0/transition_flags2 and duplicated fp temporaries in ftKp_SpecialS.c.
Search whether it_802ACBA0 receives a semantic item-spawn name after being exposed in itkoopaflame.h.
Compare later splits.txt changes to see if the SpecialLw-named functions at the SpecialN boundary are moved or intentionally retained.
